context.primary_goal: Implement the roadmap end-to-end so simulations run reliably for AVR, ESP32, and RP2040 in the FUNDI app, with ESP32 and RP2040 no longer compile-only.

execution_rules.rule: Follow the roadmap phases in order. Do not skip foundational refactor phases.
Keep changes minimal and production-safe. Preserve AVR behavior while introducing abstractions.
Do not break existing compile APIs; extend them for simulation metadata where required.
Use feature flags for incremental rollout of RP2040 and ESP32 simulation engines.
After each major phase, run targeted tests and document pass/fail with exact command outputs.
When uncertain, choose the simplest implementation that still satisfies the roadmap and acceptance criteria.

required_deliverables.deliverable: Engine abstraction extracted from hooks/useSimulation.ts into a pluggable adapter model (AVR first, no behavior regression).
Net/pin graph bridge introduced so peripheral models are board-agnostic and not AVR-port coupled.
RP2040 simulation engine adapter implemented (worker-based) and integrated with existing run/stop/reset and serial UX.
ESP32 backend simulation service implemented (session manager + emulator runner + websocket event stream) and frontend adapter connected.
Board capability matrix documented and surfaced in docs with known limitations.
Automated tests expanded to cover multi-board simulation paths, with AVR parity retained.

phase.name: Phase 0 - Baseline
phase.requirement: Lock AVR baseline behavior with parity tests before refactor.
Capture baseline simulation performance and serial output behavior.

phase.name: Phase 1 - Engine Abstraction
phase.requirement: Create SimulationEngine interfaces and AVR adapter without behavior changes.
Refactor useSimulation orchestration to select engine by board family.

phase.name: Phase 2 - Net Bridge
phase.requirement: Implement canonical pin/net model and migrate priority peripherals.
Maintain compatibility bridge for any remaining AVR-specific bindings.

phase.name: Phase 3 - RP2040
phase.requirement: Enable Pico compile-to-sim execution path with GPIO and UART functional.

phase.name: Phase 4 - ESP32
phase.requirement: Enable ESP32 compile-to-sim execution path via backend emulator sessions.
Support session lifecycle start/stop/reset and stable serial streaming.

phase.name: Phase 5 - Hardening
phase.requirement: Add diagnostics, capability matrix, and regression safety checks.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: AVR simulation tests still pass after refactor.
RP2040 sample sketch runs in simulation with visible pin interaction and serial output.
ESP32 sample sketch runs in simulation session with serial output and stable controls.
No fatal regressions in existing component simulations (I2C/SPI/UART basics preserved).
Documentation updated with architecture, limits, and run instructions.

output_format.item: Provide a concise implementation report at the end with: files changed, tests run, results, open risks, and next recommended milestone.
If blocked, provide exact blocker, attempted mitigation, and smallest unblocking next step.
